description: Interpretive coordination model describing how roles, commitments, and constraints interact across Ethereum coordination domains.

domains.name: Order Flow Access & Routing
domains.description: Access to transaction flow, routing logic, and user intent entry points.

domains.name: Solver Coordination
domains.description: Coordination of execution strategies and intent resolution across competing solvers.

domains.name: Payload Construction
domains.description: Aggregation of transactions and execution outcomes into candidate block payloads.

domains.name: Proposer-Builder Separation
domains.description: Protocol-level separation of block construction and proposal (ePBS).

domains.name: Inclusion Enforcement
domains.description: Constraint mechanisms ensuring transaction inclusion and censorship resistance.

domains.name: Commitment & Preconfirmation
domains.description: Coordination of early execution guarantees and commitment signaling.

domains.name: Finality
domains.description: Consensus-level confirmation and irreversibility of state (SSF research direction).

domains.name: Execution Coordination (Ambiguous)
domains.description: Non-canonical coordination surface spanning routing, solver activity, and payload construction.

notes: This model is interpretive and does not represent protocol execution order.
Domains may overlap and interact dynamically.
Some domains represent transitional or ambiguous terminology surfaces.
Builder markets are modeled as a legacy abstraction within payload construction.
Execution coordination is intentionally classified as ambiguous and non-canonical.
